attacking_style: Rarely attacks for personal gain; when in breakaways, shows willingness but insufficient finishing power to capitalize. Fifth-place climbing sprint at Vuelta 2022 suggests emerging punch on steep finishes.
team_tactics: Primary role as final mountain domestique for GC leaders (Carapaz, Bernal). Sets tempo in final 5km of summit finishes, typically last support before leader isolates. Part of thin Ineos climbing depth requiring relay pacing with other domestiques.
terrain_preference: High-altitude sustained climbs where he can set tempo. Exposed on technical descents (crashed/pedal-strike twice) and appears vulnerable when repeated accelerations required rather than steady pacing.
vulnerability: Mechanical issues at critical moments (3 documented incidents causing energy waste or neutralized positions). Descending technical errors. Collapses when team faces systematic high-tempo destruction. Poor form periods significantly impact effectiveness.
key_patterns: Mechanical failures at race-critical moments documented three times across 2021-2022 (chains, crashes).
Functions as last domestique on final climbs, pulls off 5km or less from summit.
Crashes and technical errors on descents repeatedly compromise team tactics and positioning.
Form deficits coincide with entire Ineos team collapses, suggesting shared training/preparation issues.
Protected GC rider role abandoned early in stage races due to time losses (2021 Giro).
form_trajectory: neutral: 2022 Vuelta showed unexpected climbing sprint ability (5th place), but overall pattern remains high-altitude pacing domestique rather than emerging GC threat. Role consistency suggests acceptance of support position.
built_from: 2021 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 7
